history.content:
<thought>
I am facing right (`>`) and there is lava directly ahead, so moving forward would be unsafe. I need to go down first into the open path, so I will turn right to face down.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am now facing down (`v`) with an open cell directly ahead, while the goal is still below and to the right. Moving forward will take me into the open corridor, which is the safest way to progress.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am in the left-side corridor facing down, and the space directly ahead is open. Continuing downward keeps me moving toward the lower part of the map and closer to the goal area, so I will move forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am still facing down and the cell ahead is open, so moving forward is the best way to continue descending toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am now on the row above a clear path to the right, but I am facing down into a non-goal area. To head toward the goal in the bottom-right, I should start turning right so I can move east along the open corridor.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing left, which is the wrong direction for reaching the goal on the bottom-right. I need to rotate clockwise to face downward first, so I can then proceed toward the open space and ultimately the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing up, so I need to rotate clockwise to face right toward the open corridor leading to the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am now facing right. The cell directly ahead is lava, so I should not move forward there. Instead, I need to turn clockwise to face down, which is the safer direction toward the open path below.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing down, and the cell ahead is open floor. Moving forward should bring me closer to the lower open corridor toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am facing down and the cell ahead is a lava tile, so moving forward would be unsafe. I should turn right to face the open path to the east.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am now facing left on the same row. The path to the goal lies to the right through the open corridor, so I need to turn around by turning left twice eventually; first I will turn left to face down, then continue reorienting toward the open route.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing down again. Moving forward would hit lava, so I need to reorient toward the clear corridor leading right toward the goal. Turning left will face me rightward.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing right and there is open space directly ahead along the corridor, which moves me closer to the goal. I should advance forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I’m in the same corridor facing right, and the path ahead is still clear. Moving forward continues toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am still facing right and the corridor ahead remains open. The goal is to the lower-right, so moving forward is the best next step.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am facing right and there is still open space directly ahead, while the goal is further to the lower-right. I should keep advancing forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am facing right, and moving forward would put me into the lava at the next cell. To avoid that, I need to turn downward toward the open path that leads to the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am now facing down. The cell directly ahead is open, so I should continue downward to progress toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am facing down and the cell ahead is lava, so moving forward would be unsafe. The open route toward the goal is to my right, so I should turn right to face left? Wait, from facing down, turning right would face left, which is not the open path. Turning left from down faces right, which is the safer direction toward the open cells near the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing right, and the goal is down-right from here. The cell directly ahead is lava, so I should avoid moving forward and instead turn to face down toward the open path leading to the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing down, and moving forward would step onto lava. To reach the goal, I need to head toward the open space to the right, so I should turn left to face right.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am back facing right. The cell ahead is lava, so I should not move forward. The open route to the goal is below and to the right, so I need to turn down again and then proceed around the obstacle.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing down again, and the cell directly ahead is lava. Turning right would face me toward the open passage on the right, which is the only safe direction to continue toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am now facing left, but the path to the goal is to the right side. From left-facing, turning left will make me face down, and then I can continue around the lava toward the open space below.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I’m facing down again, and moving forward would go into a wall. I need to turn to face the open corridor toward the goal, so I will turn right.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>
